question: How often should most people exfoliate their face?
options: Every single day for best results
Two to three times a week is enough for most skin types
Once a month is plenty
Only when skin feels very rough

question: What is the main purpose of a facial toner?
options: To deeply cleanse and remove makeup
To balance skin pH and prep skin to absorb the next skincare products
To replace moisturiser in a routine
To exfoliate dead skin cells

question: What is exfoliation?
options: Adding moisture back into your skin
Removing dead skin cells from the surface of your skin
Applying a protective layer over your skin
Cleansing your skin with a foaming product

question: What are the two main types of exfoliants?
options: Liquid and cream exfoliants
Chemical and physical exfoliants
Natural and synthetic exfoliants
Gentle and harsh exfoliants

question: What is a physical exfoliant?
options: An acid that dissolves dead skin cells
A product that uses small particles or a textured surface to manually scrub away dead skin
A serum that speeds up skin cell turnover
A toner that chemically removes dead skin

question: What is a chemical exfoliant?
options: A harsh scrub with microbeads
An acid or enzyme that dissolves the bonds holding dead skin cells together
A physical scrub with sugar particles
A cleanser with exfoliating beads

question: What does AHA stand for in skincare?
options: Active Hydrating Agent
Alpha Hydroxy Acid
Anti Hydration Acid
All Healing Antioxidant

question: What does BHA stand for in skincare?
options: Beta Hydrating Agent
Brightening Hydroxy Acid
Beta Hydroxy Acid
Base Healing Antioxidant

question: Which exfoliant type is better for treating blackheads and oily skin?
options: AHA alpha hydroxy acid
Physical scrub
BHA beta hydroxy acid like salicylic acid which is oil soluble and gets inside pores
Sugar scrub

question: Which exfoliant type is better for dry or sensitive skin because it works on the skin surface?
options: BHA salicylic acid
Walnut shell scrub
Benzoyl peroxide
AHA like lactic acid which is gentler and works on the surface

question: What is glycolic acid and what does it do?
options: A gentle moisturising acid that hydrates skin
An AHA acid with small molecules that exfoliates deeply to brighten and smooth skin texture
A BHA acid that targets oily skin and blackheads
A vitamin acid that brightens dark spots

question: What is lactic acid known for in exfoliation?
options: Being the strongest acid available for skin
Being a gentler AHA that also hydrates making it great for dry or sensitive skin
Being a BHA that clears acne and blackheads
Being an enzyme exfoliant from tropical fruits

question: What happens if you over-exfoliate your skin?
options: Skin becomes perfectly smooth and glowing
Skin barrier gets damaged causing redness irritation increased sensitivity and breakouts
Skin becomes permanently softer
Over-exfoliation only affects dry skin types

question: What are the signs that you have been over-exfoliating?
options: Skin looks plump and dewy
Skin feels tight shiny raw red and unusually sensitive or reactive
Skin looks matte and clear
Skin feels oily and thick

question: Where does toner go in a skincare routine?
options: Before cleanser as the first step
After moisturiser as the last step
After cleanser but before serum and moisturiser
Mixed with your moisturiser before applying

question: What is the difference between an old-style astringent toner and a modern hydrating toner?
options: They are exactly the same product
Old astringent toners often contained high alcohol to strip skin while modern toners tend to be hydrating and gentle
Modern toners are always stronger than old toners
Old toners were only for dry skin and new toners are only for oily skin

question: What are exfoliating toners?
options: Toners that contain heavy moisturising ingredients
Toners that contain chemical exfoliants like AHAs or BHAs to gently exfoliate with each use
Toners that physically scrub the skin when applied
Toners that only work on sensitive skin

question: How should you apply toner?
options: Rub it in hard using the palms
Splash it directly from the bottle onto your face
Apply with a cotton pad or pat gently into skin with clean hands
Apply it before cleanser to prep skin

question: What is an enzyme exfoliant and where do the enzymes come from?
options: A harsh acid exfoliant from citrus fruits
A gentle exfoliant that uses natural enzymes often from fruits like papaya or pineapple to dissolve dead skin
A physical scrub made from fruit seeds
A toner that brightens skin using fruit extracts

question: What fruit enzyme is commonly used in gentle exfoliating masks?
options: Apple enzyme
Papaya enzyme called papain or pineapple enzyme called bromelain
Lemon enzyme
Banana enzyme

question: Should you use a chemical exfoliant and a physical scrub on the same day?
options: Yes combining them gives the best results
No using both on the same day is too much and can damage the skin barrier
Only if you have oily skin
Only if you use gentle versions of both

question: What should you always use after exfoliating to protect your skin?
options: Another round of exfoliation to maximise results
Sunscreen because exfoliation increases sun sensitivity
Heavy oil to seal in results
A strong active serum to penetrate the fresh skin

question: What is a konjac sponge used for in skincare?
options: Applying chemical exfoliants to skin
Gentle physical cleansing and very mild exfoliation with a soft natural texture
Removing eye makeup thoroughly
Applying toner evenly across the face

question: What does pH mean and why does it matter for toners?
options: pH measures the thickness of a product
pH measures acidity and toners help restore the skin's natural slightly acidic pH after cleansing
pH measures how hydrating a product is
pH only matters for cleansers not toners

question: What is the natural pH level of healthy skin?
options: Highly alkaline around pH 9 to 10
Neutral at exactly pH 7
Slightly acidic around pH 4.5 to 5.5
Highly acidic around pH 2 to 3

question: Why does cleansing sometimes raise skin pH and why does toner help?
options: Cleansers lower pH and toners raise it back up
Many cleansers are alkaline and can raise skin pH so toners help bring it back down to its healthy acidic level
Cleansing has no effect on skin pH
Toners raise skin pH to make it more alkaline

question: What is a sugar scrub?
options: A sweet smelling toner
A physical exfoliant that uses sugar granules to manually buff away dead skin
A chemical exfoliant with sugar-derived acids
A hydrating mask with natural sugars

question: Why are walnut shell or apricot kernel scrubs controversial in skincare?
options: Because they are too gentle to be effective
Because irregular sharp edges can cause micro tears in the skin which can be irritating
Because they are only suitable for body not face
Because they leave an oily residue on skin

question: What is a gentler alternative to rough scrubs for physical exfoliation?
options: More aggressive acid exfoliants
A soft muslin cloth or silicone cleansing brush used gently
Sandpaper or rough textured gloves
Baking soda scrub

question: How long should you leave a chemical exfoliant on your skin before rinsing?
options: Overnight for best results
Follow the product instructions as leave-on and rinse-off formulas vary significantly
Exactly 30 seconds then rinse
At least one hour

question: What is the difference between a leave-on and rinse-off exfoliant?
options: Leave-on exfoliants are always stronger than rinse-off ones
Leave-on exfoliants stay on skin to work continuously while rinse-off ones are washed off after a short time
Rinse-off exfoliants always work better
Leave-on exfoliants are only for body use

question: What is a toning essence popular in Korean skincare?
options: A very thick cream toner for dry skin
A lightweight watery product that hydrates and preps skin often packed with fermented ingredients
A strong chemical exfoliant in toner form
A toner that contains SPF for morning routines

question: Which skin type should exfoliate the least frequently?
options: Oily skin
Normal skin
Combination skin
Dry or sensitive skin which can be easily irritated by frequent exfoliation

question: What is the 7 skin method from Korean skincare?
options: Using 7 different toners
Applying toner 7 times in thin layers to deeply hydrate skin
Exfoliating 7 days a week
Using 7 steps of chemical exfoliation

question: Can you use an exfoliating toner every day?
options: Yes daily exfoliating toners are always fine for everyone
It depends on the concentration and your skin type but most people should use them just a few times a week
Daily exfoliating toners are only for oily skin
Exfoliating toners should only be used once a month

question: What does a toner do for large-looking pores?
options: Permanently shrinks pores
Some toners with niacinamide or mild exfoliants can help minimise the appearance of pores over time
Toners have no effect on pore appearance
Toners make pores more visible

question: What is the best way to exfoliate if you have active acne?
options: Use the strongest scrub available to clear breakouts
Choose a gentle BHA like salicylic acid and avoid harsh physical scrubs that can spread bacteria
Avoid all exfoliation completely if you have any acne
Exfoliate twice daily until acne clears

question: What ingredient makes witch hazel a popular toner ingredient?
options: It contains AHA acids for exfoliation
It is a natural astringent with anti-inflammatory properties that can calm and tighten skin
It contains hyaluronic acid for hydration
It contains retinol for anti-aging benefits

question: Is alcohol-based toner good or bad for your skin?
options: Always great for all skin types
High alcohol content can strip skin's natural oils and disrupt the skin barrier so it is generally not recommended
Alcohol toners are only bad for dry skin
Alcohol toners are the best option for oily skin

question: What is a gommage exfoliant?
options: A French word for a strong chemical peel
A gentle exfoliant that is applied and then rolled off the skin taking dead skin cells with it
A physical scrub made from coffee grounds
A toner with AHA and BHA combined

question: Should you use a toner with retinol in your routine?
options: No retinol should never be near a toner
An exfoliating toner and retinol should not be used on the same night to avoid over-exfoliating but a hydrating toner is fine
Toners and retinol should always be used together
Toners always make retinol more effective

question: What is the correct frequency for using a strong AHA like glycolic acid at 10 percent or higher?
options: Twice daily for fast results
Once or twice a week maximum to avoid over-exfoliating
Every single day
Only once a year as a treatment

question: What does mandelic acid offer that makes it good for darker skin tones when exfoliating?
options: It is the strongest acid for fast results
It has larger molecules that penetrate slowly making it gentler and less likely to cause post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation
It specifically targets melanin to brighten all skin tones
It is the only acid safe for darker skin tones

question: What is a hydrating toner best known for giving skin?
options: A tingly clean feeling
A boost of moisture and a dewy fresh feeling after cleansing
A matte oil-free finish
A strong exfoliating effect

question: What does rose water toner do for skin?
options: Provides strong chemical exfoliation
Soothes and hydrates skin with a gentle calming effect and light natural fragrance
Deeply cleanses pores like a BHA
Provides anti-aging benefits like retinol

question: What is the main benefit of exfoliation for people with dull skin?
options: It adds more oil to dry dull skin
It removes the layer of dead skin cells that is making skin look dull revealing brighter fresher skin underneath
It permanently changes dull skin to glowing skin
It only helps dull skin in winter months

question: When should you stop using an exfoliant or toner immediately?
options: When your skin starts to feel slightly smooth
When you notice persistent redness burning peeling or increased breakouts that do not go away
When the product is half empty
When you start a new moisturiser

question: What is the difference between a scrub and a peel?
options: They are exactly the same product
A scrub uses physical particles to manually remove dead skin while a peel uses chemicals or enzymes to dissolve dead skin
Peels are always stronger than scrubs
Scrubs are only for the body and peels are only for the face

question: Why should you avoid using exfoliants on sunburned or compromised skin?
options: Because exfoliants will make the tan disappear faster
Because exfoliating already damaged or irritated skin will worsen the damage and cause more pain and sensitivity
Because exfoliants and sun damage cancel each other out
Because sunburned skin absorbs too much product

question: What is the correct patch test process before using a new exfoliant or toner?
options: Apply it all over your face immediately to test it
Apply a small amount to a discreet area like behind the ear or inner wrist and wait 24 hours to check for a reaction
Only patch test if you have sensitive skin
Patch testing is unnecessary for gentle products
